==
“Indoor Graveyard”
==


'''Toggle Vignette'''
'''Toggle Vignette'''

=== Content Warning === This page contains descriptions of '''suicide, and suicidal horror''' that may be distressing or unsuitable for some readers. Viewer discretion is strongly advised.
{{Class Ψ|color-h=0|color-s=0%|color-l=50%}} ----
| Overview
{{Simple Image Block|image=Btagol suic.png|caption=''He's alone. Why not be his caring friend…?''|style=border: none;|align=right}} '''Level 237''', otherwise designated as '''''“Indoor Graveyard,”''''' is the '''238th''' level of the Backrooms. Upon entry, the environment does not initially manifest as a coherent structure, instead as an expansive void densely filled with innumerable clusters of vibrant, and swirling particles. These particles move choatically throughout the space, and exhibit no discernible pattern or consistent trajectory. During this phase, gravity is entirely absent, which causes individuals to drift purposelessly within the abyss; devoid of orientation. After approximately one minute, the particles begin to converge and organize in the vicinity of the individual. Their motion becomes smooth and deliberate, opposing their prior choatic behaviours. Progressively, they group into defined, and sharp formations that establish the traversable layout of the level. Upon completion of this process, gravitational forces, physical solidity, and environmental stability are fully restored. {{Simple Image Block|image=Btagol lvl.png|caption=''Look at them: They are happy like in your childhood…''|style=border: none;|align=left}} '''Level 237''' manifests as an enclosed household complex composed of narrow corridors, intersections, and largely barren rooms. Illumination throughout the environment is minimal, inefficient, and inconsistent, which often makes distant areas obscured. Furnishings and decorative objects are scarce; the ones that do appear are usually in a severely deteriorated, and aged condition. The majority of the walls are covered with decaying wallpaper in murky green or brown colours, while the rest of them are painted in a dull, and muted yellow paint. The flooring varies in elevation, texture, and generally architecture, yet consistently has a sombre bluish color. Unlike those room elements, the ceiling stays static and unchanging not only in structure but also in appearance, and it has the same monotonous colour as the painted walls. A defining anomaly of '''Level 237''' is the pervasive presence of deceased human figures hanging from ceilings, doorframes, and various suspended structures. These bodies exist in differing stages of decomposition, and lack any identifiable features or recoverable identification. It is currently hypothesized that these figures do not originate from external sources, but are, instead, strangely generated by the level itself. The placements of these bodies are both random and densely cluttered. Entire corridors are often filled with body accumulations that block navigation, and, in certain occasions, stairways end in a single suspended body. {{Simple Image Block|image=Btagol narrow.png|caption=''Isn't it depressing to be alone…?''|style=border: none;|align=right}} The condition of these bodies contributes significantly to the hazardous environment. Decomposed bodies emit an unbearable, and mixed stench of rotten organic matter and oxidized blood. Individuals often described this odor as overwhelming and persistent regardless of one's location within the level. Furthermore, these manifestations are theorised to be linked to a simple phenomenon of the level, which appear to cause severe physiological and psychological stress on individuals over time. There are no confirmed reports of multiple individuals encountering one another within '''Level 237''', which means [[Phenomenon 15]] affects the level and is further supported by the fact of the level forming in seconds upon entry. This loneliness negatively contributes to the depressing environment of the level, making individuals susceptible to the effects of [[Phenomenon 25]] and [[Phenomenon 35]]. Although, despite this, there are no confirmed sightings of encountering [[Phenomenon 35|Wretches]]. ----
Entity…?
{{Simple Image Block|image=Btagol memory.png|caption=''Do you feel the warmth and nostalgia? You weren't miserable back then…''|style=border: none;|align=right}} Regions that resemble places of personal childhood with an absence of hanging bodies are known to have an anomalous presence designated as '''''“Memories' Facelings.”''''' This presence is not continuously visible; instead, it manifests only when an individual enters an indeterminate proximity; detection is therefore inconsistent and cannot be measured. Due to its conditional appearance and lack of reactive behaviours, classification is still unresolved with conflicting thoughts of whether '''Memories' Facelings''' fits best in level-exclusive entity or a phenomenon category. Once manifested, the presence appears as multiple humanoid figures with a lack of facial detail, similar to [[Entity 9|Facelings]]. These figures take forms of individuals from one's childhood, with representations of the individual at a younger age, close friends, and close relatives. Their behaviours appear to repeatedly replicate sequence of past events or routines familiar to the individual. Interactions within their own context are logical but are completely unresponsive to external interactions. ----
Narrative


Recovered Handwritten Documentary
A Glimpse of '''Life''',
Before '''Twilight'''
I tried to open my eyes, though a heavy drowsiness fought against the effort. I slipped into brief wakefulness and immediately sensed an odd sensation; I felt as I were sinking into the deep depths of a vast ocean expanse. Because of my strong fear of deep water, I jolted myself awake and forced my eyelids apart. Once my eyes finally opened, I was met with a sight I hadn't seen in a long time…
[[File:Btagol eye.png|center|link=|200px]]
[[File:Btagol eye.png|center|link=|200px]]
I had found myself whitin an endless, and vanta-black hollow; a vast pitch-black abyss. Yet, it was not entirely empty. Countless, and overwhelming clusters of colorful, and swirling particles filled the void, while they danced in a chaotic mess. And there I was, simply floating directionless among them without a clear purpose; the only true oddity in this strange space. My memory felt fractured. How did I get here? Why was I sent here? So, what had I done to deserve this isolation? What had happened before all of this nonsense? I could only recall a single, and strange moment from the recent past: I had been walking in loops through an incomprehensible labyrinth. Its walls were glitching, and its halls were built with a forsaken architecture. I remember a feeling of dread and anxiety of being lost. None of it made sense. Instead of answers, it only led more questions. '''Turn Page 1/6'''
In a sudden blink of an eye, I caught the surrounding particles rapidly shifting place to place. They all gathered around me, condensing into a form. At first, it was too chaotic to be comprehensible. But, gradually, its shape sharpened and became as a barren bedroom. The room appeared strikingly familiar. It was my bedroom I once had as a child. I expected the surfaces of the room to be intangible, but when the gravity strengthened, my toes met the floor, it was cold and stiff. I stomped to test its solidness, yet the ground did not even flinch. The floor, the ceiling, the walls, everything was rigid. I was disturbed by an awful stench that kept bleeding through my nose. I could not understand what caused this unbearable odor. I walked towards the bed, and tried to find rest and comfort. To my shock, solidity had claimed the fabric of it as well. It no longer felt fluffy; just dull, and solid as like trying to lay flat on a rough concrete surface. The door creaked open slowly. I tried to hide beneath the bed, but I had grown too big to fit. Soft children’s laughter echoed through the narrow opening. My heartbeat pounded in my ears. Something could be out there; something dangerous, and something pretending. What if it was mimicking children to lure me? Then, the laughter of the unknown faded into intelligible children conversation. I recognized what they were talking about, it was about my childhood hobby I had once been chronically obsessed with. '''Turn Page 2/6'''
The door slammed open suddenly. I hit my head accidentally in shock. Two children silhouettes rushed in. One had the shape of a little boy, and the other one had the shape of a little girl. Instinctively, I immediately stood up. But the two children seemed to ignore my presence. Their face was… wrong, they seem to lack eyes, mouth, and nose. My hand gently rose, and waved at them to see if I was actually invisible to them. They did not react, not at all. Instead, they kept playing and laughing around with a toy train. I looked closer at the train, it appeared familiar. I failed to understand. Then, the anomalies faded out into non-existence. The door closed gently by itself. But, moments later, it opened again. This time, four faceless figures entered: A boy, a girl, and their parents. A vintage television blurred into existence. The parents covered the children's non-existing eyes and began counting down from five to zero. Once the countdown reached zero, their hands lifted, and the kids reacted with surprise and joy. I felt like I knew this moment. That was when I understood: This place… was showing me my past childhood memories… But… why? The silhouettes dissolved once more, their forms unraveling into fine, and weightless dust. But the television stayed. The door creaked shut.
Then it opened again.
And again.
And again. I no longer know how to measure time anymore. Repeating the same sequence of memories without mercy. Fragments of moments I could almost recognize; birthdays, and laughter that no longer convey warmth. Something was wrong. Each memory felt thinner and less happier than its previous, like a copy of a copy that slowly degrades into sorrowful moments. The laughter started losing its sound. '''Turn Page 3/6'''
Even the colors became dull, and turned into a muted, and… lifeless gray. I stopped trying to watch what was happening. I stopped trying to understand. The memories don't feel like mine anymore. They were only movements and disturbances in the still environment. And were empty, and endlessly repetitive. I don’t know how long I’ve been here. Hunger bites me, but it never consumes me. My body aches, but it never fails. Sleep never comes, no matter how badly I need it. I am forced to be awake, aware, and trapped within a cycle that doesn't pretends to comfort me anymore. I tried to scream… But, the room did not react. And, the memories did not stop. I tried to slip through the door… But the door leads only to the void. So I stopped trying. Memories came,
then went away. Another came,
then faded just the same. And another.
And another.
And another. Until they were no longer memories at all. Just shapes moving… '''Turn Page 4/6'''
Then, for the first time in what felt like an eternity; something new happened. The room did not reform into another memory. The door did not open. A subtle creak echoed above me. I slowly lifted my head. A rope hung from the ceiling. It swayed gently with a non-existing breeze.
[[File:Btagol noose.png|center|link=|250px]]
[[File:Btagol noose.png|center|link=|250px]]
I stared at it. For a long time, I did nothing else but write this. It hadn’t been there before.
Not in any of the memories.
Not in any of the loops. Something new.
Something… really new. My chest tightened with a feeling heavier than fear and hesitation.
Relief. Finally, this place had given me something that was not a repetition. Something that was not a deception. My hand twitched. Then slowly, I reached out toward it. Trembling with exhaustion instead of fear. '''Turn Page 5/6'''
The rope continued to sway. Waiting. And for the first time since I arrived here…
I understood what I was supposed to do. A perfect gift…


















'''6/6'''
----
Entrances & Exits
=== Entrances === Individuals may regain consciousness within the multicolored abyss without any memory of the circumstances leading to their arrival to '''Level 237'''. The only consistently reported memory is a vague, and fragmented impression of wandering through an incomprehensibly distorted labyrinth of intersecting corridors that exhibit anomalous, and glitching architectures. Due to the inconsistency and unreliability of these accounts, the method of entry into the level is undetermined. === Exits === • No-clipping through a noisy, and blurry wall, may lead one to [[Level 238]].
• Exploring into an irregularly bright area, has the chance of the current level transitioning to [[Level 242]] at anytime.
